intro: Estimation and inference for causal estimands: orthogonal moments and double machine learning, influence functions and asymptotic normality, efficiency bounds, cross-fitting, CATE learners, NPIV, and rate/coverage-facing results.

namespace_intros.ATE: Sample-split AIPW/DML estimation of the back-door average treatment effect: the moment function, its influence function, square-integrability, and the asymptotic-linearity theorem.
namespace_intros.ATE.Score: AIPW score infrastructure for ATE DML: moment definitions, influence functions, finite variance, mean-zero identities, L2 continuity, and pull-out lemmas.
namespace_intros.ATT: AIPW/DML estimation of the average treatment effect on the treated (Hahn-form moment), mirroring the ATE development; the top-level theorem files are pending repair against the current Mathlib pin.
namespace_intros.ATT.Remainder: Second-order remainder analysis for the ATT AIPW moment: the exact remainder identity and its quantitative product-of-errors bound.
namespace_intros.ATT.Score: AIPW score infrastructure for ATT DML: treated-effect moments, influence functions, finite variance, mean-zero facts, L2 continuity, and arm-specific pull-out lemmas.
namespace_intros.CATE: Estimation of conditional average treatment effects: the DR-Learner, its oracle expansions, linear-smoother specializations, and orthogonal-statistical-learning analyses.
namespace_intros.CATE.Core: Core DR-Learner pseudo-outcome theory for CATE: conditional bias, directional derivatives, conditional-mean identities, and second-order bias.
namespace_intros.CATE.Kennedy: Kennedy-style DR-Learner theory: the doubly robust pseudo-outcome, its oracle expansion, and the linear-smoother error bound.
namespace_intros.CATE.OSL: The DR-Learner cast as an orthogonal statistical-learning problem: directional derivatives, localized moduli, and end-to-end oracle inequalities.
namespace_intros.CATE.OSL.DRLearner: Analytic ingredients (directional-derivative bundle) for the DR-Learner's orthogonal statistical-learning system.
namespace_intros.CATE.OSL.Modulus: Empirical-process modulus bounds powering the DR-Learner oracle inequalities: fixed and random nuisances, honest splitting, localization, and the end-to-end composition.
namespace_intros.CATE.OrthogonalLearning: DR-Learner orthogonal-learning system and oracle inequalities for CATE, including analytic derivatives, empirical-process moduli, and random-nuisance variants.
namespace_intros.CATE.OrthogonalLearning.LocalEmpProcess: Empirical-process modulus results for the CATE DR-Learner: Rademacher, localized, honest-split, random-nuisance, and end-to-end oracle bounds.
namespace_intros.DTR: DML estimation for dynamic treatment regimes: the two-period AIPW moment, remainder identities and bounds, and the asymptotic-linearity theorem.
namespace_intros.DTR.RemainderIdentity: Algebraic helpers for the exact second-order remainder identity of the dynamic-treatment-regime moment.
namespace_intros.DTR.ScoreL2: Square-integrability of the dynamic-treatment-regime score on the nuisance neighbourhood.
namespace_intros.Efficiency: Semiparametric efficiency: tangent spaces, pathwise differentiability, and the efficiency bound for the ATE functional.
namespace_intros.GaussMarkov: The Gauss-Markov theorem for linear models: best linear unbiased estimation under spherical errors.
namespace_intros.NPIV: Nonparametric instrumental variables: the conditional-moment operator, sieve primal analysis, ill-posedness measures, and doubly robust NPIV functionals.
namespace_intros.NPIV.DR: Doubly robust estimation of linear functionals of the NPIV structural function.
namespace_intros.NPIV.Operator: The NPIV conditional-expectation operator: compactness, singular systems, and the source condition.
namespace_intros.NPIV.Primal: The sieve primal NPIV analysis: projected residuals, variance and bias terms, and the rate theorem for the structural function.
namespace_intros.NPIV.Primal.EmpiricalProcessEvent: The high-probability empirical-process events (Gram-matrix control, residual concentration) underlying the sieve NPIV rate.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalLearning: Generic orthogonal statistical-learning substrate: population derivative bundles, cross-fitted plug-in ERM, local moduli, oracle inequalities, and sparse specializations.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalLearning.LocalEmpProcess: Local empirical-process modulus machinery for orthogonal learning, derived from Rademacher, localized, fixed-nuisance, and random-nuisance regimes.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalLearning.Population: Population-side orthogonal-learning definitions: directional derivatives, Neyman orthogonality, and nuisance-induced second-order bias.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalLearning.Sparse: Sparse finite-dimensional plug-in ERM theory: restricted strong convexity, restricted cones, one-norm geometry, and lasso-style oracle guarantees.
namespace_intros.OSL: Orthogonal statistical learning: directional derivatives of orthogonal losses, local empirical-process moduli, and plug-in oracle inequalities for second-stage learners.
namespace_intros.OSL.Modulus: Derivations of the local empirical-process modulus assumption from Rademacher-complexity regimes.
namespace_intros.OSL.Sparse: Sparse (lasso-type) specializations of the orthogonal statistical-learning oracle inequalities.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalMoments: Neyman-orthogonal moment functions: construction, cross-fitting, parametric examples, and automatic debiasing.
namespace_intros.OrthogonalMoments.AutoDebias: Automatic debiasing: Riesz-representer-based construction of orthogonal moments for linear functionals.
namespace_intros.PLR: Partially linear regression DML: Robinson partialling-out scores, nuisance bundles, mean-zero and remainder facts, Jacobian consistency, and one-step plus feasible asymptotic normality.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE: Structure-agnostic lower bounds for treatment-effect estimation: the oracle-vs-black-box reduction, least-favourable constructions, and variance calculations.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Achievability: Achievability side of the structure-agnostic program: estimators matching the lower-bound rate.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Base: Base layer of the structure-agnostic framework: the model class, black-box nuisance access, and the reduction interfaces.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Causal: Causal grounding for the MinimaxATE lower bounds: a finite backdoor SCM and potential-outcome system, the causalATE target, and bridges that recenter the lower bound from the observed-data contrast to the causal ATE.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.ConstCenterGeneral: General constant-center least-favourable construction for MinimaxATE: mixture laws, class membership, χ² overlap, gap, MSE, and lower-bound assembly.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.ConstCenterHalf: Half-center constant-propensity lower-bound construction for MinimaxATE: explicit witnesses, χ² indistinguishability, Ingster-style calibration, and parametric variants.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Gen: Generic (estimand-agnostic) pieces of the structure-agnostic lower-bound argument.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Reduction: The reduction from estimation risk to testing between mixture alternatives, including witness-mixture constructions.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Var: Variance computations for the first least-favourable pair in the structure-agnostic lower bound.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.Var2: Variance computations for the second least-favourable pair in the structure-agnostic lower bound.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.VaryingCenterCase1: First varying-center MinimaxATE lower-bound construction: cell-varying mixture laws, class membership, χ² overlap, ATE gap, and risk lower bound.
namespace_intros.MinimaxATE.VaryingCenterCase2: Second, propensity-dominant varying-center MinimaxATE construction: perturbed sample laws, positivity algebra, class membership, χ² overlap, and lower-bound gap.
